title: Mustardy Fish Tiradito
description: A diabetic-friendly Peruvian dish featuring fish tiradito in a mustardy sauce, served in a cataplana with rice paper sheets. This recipe is a comforting, nostalgic take on traditional Peruvian cuisine.
instructions: In a blender or food processor, combine rice vinegar, Dijon mustard, garlic, and lemon grass. Blend until smooth.
Heat the olive oil in a saucepan over medium heat.
Pour the blended sauce into the saucepan and bring to a simmer.
Add the fish sauce and cook for 1 minute.
In a separate pan, heat the fish over medium heat until cooked through.
Cut the cooked fish into thin strips and place in a serving dish.
Soak the rice paper sheets in cold water for 5 minutes, then remove and place on a clean surface.
Place a few strips of fish in the center of each rice paper sheet.
Add a spoonful of the mustard sauce on top of the fish, followed by some chopped green onion and parsley.
Fold the bottom half of the rice paper sheet up over the filling, then fold in the sides and roll up the sheet to form a tight cylinder.
Repeat with the remaining ingredients and serve in a cataplana.
Cover the cataplana with rice paper sheets and serve hot.
This dish is best served immediately, as the rice paper sheets will begin to break down quickly.
If serving later, cover with plastic wrap and refrigerate for up to 2 hours.
Serve with steamed vegetables or a side salad for a well-rounded meal.
Reheat the mustard sauce in a saucepan over low heat before serving.
If desired, garnish with additional chopped green onion and parsley.
